nodes.label: Multi-Tenant SaaS Architecture
nodes.rationale: Strict per-firm data isolation. Every query is filtered by tenantId at the app layer using an AsyncLocalStorage context plus Prisma Client Extensions, rather than relying solely on DB row-level security.

nodes.label: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
nodes.rationale: Three roles (admin, lawyer, paralegal) gate sensitive/state-changing endpoints via the @Roles decorator and RolesGuard.

nodes.label: JWT Authentication
nodes.rationale: Signed access + refresh tokens with configurable expiry provide stateless, secure session management.

nodes.label: Real-Time Notifications
nodes.rationale: Socket.io pushes instant updates on case changes and task assignments to connected firm users.

nodes.label: Urgency Alert System
nodes.rationale: Critical high-priority alerts bypass the standard notification queue to trigger instant UI pop-ups for imminent deadlines.

nodes.label: Automated Hearing Reminders
nodes.rationale: NestJS Cron jobs scan for hearings 3 days in advance and notify the firm.

nodes.label: Audit Trail
nodes.rationale: Immutable logging of every INSERT/UPDATE on cases for full traceability and compliance.

nodes.label: Document Management System (DMS)
nodes.rationale: Category-grouped legal documents (Evidence, Acts, Correspondence) stored in MinIO (S3-compatible) and served via secure presigned URLs.

nodes.label: AI RAG Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Retrieval-Augmented Generation over case files. The docs cite Gemini 1.5 Pro, but the implementation routes through N8nRagService + a Qdrant vector DB and returns answers with page citations.

nodes.label: Profitability Engine
nodes.rationale: A persistent global timer records billable hours; invoices are generated automatically from tracked time entries.

nodes.label: CRM (Client Management)
nodes.rationale: Centralized directory of physical and legal entities with full contact, case, and document history.

nodes.label: Case & Hearing Management
nodes.rationale: Full lifecycle tracking of legal proceedings with an O(1)-optimized calendar view and real-time hearing reminders.

nodes.label: SQL Injection Protection
nodes.rationale: Use Prisma's parameterized methods (findMany/create/etc). If raw SQL is unavoidable use $queryRaw/$executeRaw template literals; never $queryRawUnsafe with interpolated user input.

nodes.label: Tiered Rate Limiting
nodes.rationale: Maintain short/medium/long throttler tiers in AppModule; apply stricter @Throttle limits to authentication and registration endpoints.

nodes.label: Security Headers & TLS
nodes.rationale: helmet() must be applied in main.ts and production must redirect to HTTPS.

nodes.label: Tenant-Bound Model Registration
nodes.rationale: Every new Prisma model in schema.prisma must also be added to TENANT_BOUND_MODELS in prisma.service.ts so the extension auto-filters it by tenantId.

nodes.label: Active-User WebSocket Check
nodes.rationale: Before establishing or maintaining a WebSocket connection, verify the user is still isActive in the database.

nodes.label: Design System Component Library
nodes.rationale: Shared Button/Badge/Card/Input/Modal primitives with defined variants and sizes for a consistent UI.

nodes.label: WCAG Contrast Compliance
nodes.rationale: All text must meet WCAG AA 4.5:1 (AAA 7:1 preferred). Never use slate-500 for body text - use slate-600 or darker.

nodes.label: Accessibility Standards
nodes.rationale: aria-labels on buttons, role=dialog on modals, visible :focus-visible indicators, >=44x44px touch targets, and aria-live announcements for loading states.
